debug_util(3) Library Functions Manual debug_util(3)

NAME

debug_util - Debug_util

SYNOPSIS

Files


file backtrace.c
📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting implementation file memory.c
🧠 Memory debugging implementation for ascii-chat debug builds file backtrace.h
📍 Backtrace capture, symbolization, and formatting

Functions


void backtrace_print_symbols (const char *label, char **symbols, int count, int skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)
Print pre-resolved backtrace symbols with consistent formatting. void backtrace_log_frames (int num_frames, int skip_frames)
Log N backtrace frames with function name, line number, and file. void platform_install_crash_handler (void)
Install crash handlers for the application.

Detailed Description

Provides a unified backtrace_t type and API for capturing, symbolizing, and printing stack traces. This abstraction consolidates backtrace handling that was previously scattered across multiple files.

Purpose

Stack traces are invaluable for understanding program flow during:

  • Development: Debugging segfaults, assertion failures, and unexpected state
  • Production: Correlating errors with their root causes in logs/reports
  • Testing: Validating execution paths and error conditions

This module provides efficient capture (single syscall), optional symbolization (maps addresses to function names/source locations), and flexible printing (colored terminal vs. plain log file formats).

Key Features

  • Platform abstraction: POSIX (backtrace/addr2line) and Windows (StackWalk) support
  • Efficient capture: Minimal overhead - single platform_backtrace() call
  • Lazy symbolization: Only symbolize when needed, not on every capture
  • Frame filtering: Skip internal/framework frames to focus on application code
  • Dual output: Colored stderr for development, plain text for log files
  • Multiple formats: Print to stderr, format to buffer, or batch-print multiple traces

Usage Examples

Development: Quick Backtrace on Error

asciichat_error_t result = some_operation();
if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    backtrace_t bt;


    backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);


    backtrace_print("Error backtrace", &bt, 0, 5, NULL);


    backtrace_t_free(&bt);


    return result;
}

Production: Memory Leak with Source Locations

// During allocation - store backtrace for leak reporting
backtrace_t alloc_trace;
backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&alloc_trace);
// ... store trace in allocation metadata ...
// On leak detection (exit time):
backtrace_print("Leaked from", &alloc_trace, 0, 0, NULL);

Filtering: Skip Framework Frames

bool skip_internal_frames(const char *frame) {


    // Skip lib/platform and lib/log frames


    return strstr(frame, "lib/platform") != NULL ||


           strstr(frame, "lib/log") != NULL;
}
backtrace_t bt;
backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
backtrace_print("Application trace", &bt, 0, 10, skip_internal_frames);

Buffer Formatting: Log-Safe Output

backtrace_t bt;
backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
char buffer[4096];
int written = backtrace_format(buffer, sizeof(buffer),


                               "Trace", &bt, 0, 0, NULL);
if (written > 0) {


    log_info("Stack: %s", buffer);  // Safe for log system
}
backtrace_t_free(&bt);

Build Modes

  • Full support: Linux with addr2line, macOS, Windows with DWARF/PDB info
  • Limited support: Embedded systems may have symbolic info stripped
  • Graceful degradation: Hex addresses are always available even if symbols fail

See also

lib/platform/backtrace.h for platform-specific implementations

asciichat_errno.h for backtrace_t structure definition

Function Documentation

void backtrace_log_frames (int num_frames, int skip_frames)

#include <include/ascii-chat/debug/backtrace.h>

Log N backtrace frames with function name, line number, and file. Captures and logs the backtrace at debug level, showing the function name, file path, and line number for each frame.

Parameters

num_frames Number of frames to capture and log (0 = all available)
skip_frames Number of frames to skip from the top (e.g., skip this function itself)

Example output: [0] my_function() at src/main.c:42 [1] caller_function() at src/caller.c:123 [2] main() at src/main.c:999

Note

Uses log_debug() for output

Call with skip_frames=1 to skip the platform_log_backtrace_frames() call itself

Definition at line 393 of file debug/backtrace.c.

393                                                            {
394   backtrace_t bt;
395   backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(&bt);
396   backtrace_print("Backtrace", &bt, skip_frames, num_frames, NULL);
397   backtrace_t_free(&bt);
398 }




References backtrace_capture_and_symbolize(),
    backtrace_print(), and backtrace_t_free().






void
  backtrace_print_symbols (const char * label, char ** symbols, int count, int
  skip_frames, int max_frames, backtrace_frame_filter_t filter)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/debug/backtrace.h>


Print pre-resolved backtrace symbols with consistent formatting.
    Uses colored format for all backtraces: [-1]
    crypto_handshake_server_complete() (lib/crypto/handshake.c:1471) [0]
    server_crypto_handshake() (src/server/crypto.c:511)




Parameters


label Header label (e.g., "Backtrace",
  "Call stack")


symbols Array of pre-resolved symbol strings


count Number of symbols in the array


skip_frames Number of frames to skip from the start


max_frames Maximum frames to print (-1 = unlimited)


filter Optional filter callback to skip specific frames (NULL = no
  filtering)


Definition at line 400 of file
  debug/backtrace.c.


401                                                               {
402   backtrace_t bt = {0};
403   bt.symbols = symbols;
404   bt.count = count;
405   backtrace_print(label, &bt, skip_frames, max_frames, filter);
406 }






References backtrace_print(), backtrace_t::count,
    and backtrace_t::symbols.






void
  platform_install_crash_handler (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/platform/system.h>


Install crash handlers for the application. Installs signal
    handlers for common crash signals (SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, etc.) that will print a
    backtrace before terminating the process.




Note


This should be called early in program
  initialization.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
